title: Novavax Going Concern: From Pandemic Success to Financial Uncertainty and Strategic Pivot
text: Novavax disclosed substantial doubt regarding its ability to continue as a going concern through one year from the date of its early 2023 financial statements. This critical disclosure highlighted the dramatic reversal of fortune for a company that had been a pandemic-era success story. The going concern warning reflected the company's challenges in transitioning from pandemic-era revenues to sustainable long-term business models in a maturing COVID vaccine market. As of year-end 2025, Novavax has stabilized its balance sheet with a cash runway extending into late 2027, a significant improvement from the going concern warnings of early 2023. Once teetering on the edge of bankruptcy, Novavax has effectively reinvented itself from a struggling standalone manufacturer into a lean, high-margin licensing and platform company. Under the hood, Novavax still carries negative equity but a strong liquidity cushion: about $795M in cash and short-term investments plus a new $330M credit facility. The company resolved its critical going concern issue through improved cash management and strategic partnerships, most notably with Sanofi. The turnaround from going concern warning to profitability in 2025 represents one of biotech's more dramatic transformations in recent years.
